MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-16-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS IGNACIO CAMARILLO, JOHN VAN NOORD, DONNA VAN NOORD AND CHRISTIANE GIGAS' DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. IF IT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, PLAINTIFF 777 PLUMBING, INC. IS GRANTED LEAVE TO FILE A NEW THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION LABELED "QUANTUM MERUIT AND UNJUST ENRICHMENT" ALLEGING THAT, WITH THE KNOWLEDGE OF DEFENDANTS PROPERTY OWNERS, IT PROVIDED CONSTRUCTION SERVICES AND FURNISHED CONSTRUCTION MATERIALS WHICH BENEFITTED THE PROPERTY OF THE PROPERTY OWNERS, 777 PLUMBING WAS NOT PAID FOR SOME OF THOSE SERVICES AND MATERIALS AND THE PROPERTY OWNERS DID NOT PAY FOR THE SERVICES AND MATERIALS FOR WHICH 777 PLUMBING HAS NOT BEEN PAID. THESE ALLEGATIONS SUFFICIENTLY STATE A CLAIM AND WOULD WITHSTAND A DEMURRER. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS IGNACIO CAMARILLO, JOHN VAN NOORD, DONNA VAN NOORD AND CHRISTIANE GIGAS' MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT AND EVERY CAUSE OF ACTION AND ALLEGATION THERIN AGAINST DEFENDANTS JOHN VAN NOORD, DONNA VAN NOORD AND CHRISTIANE GIGAS - DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. A MOTION TO STRIKE IS NOT THE PROPER PROCEDURE TO SEEK ADJUDICATION OF AN ENTIRE CLAIM, IN ALL EVENTS, THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS DUPLICATIVE OF THE DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christopher Land, Esq. (415-788-8100) of Law Offices of John Kithas for Plaintiff 777 Plumbing Inc.
Mark J. Rice, Esq. (415-472-3434) of Law Offices of McNeil, Silveria, Rice & Wiley for Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS IGNACIO CAMARILLO, DONNA VAN NOORD and CHRISTIANE GIGAS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is GRANTED. There is nothing procedurally improper by the owner defendants and general contractor defendant filing a joint motion for summary judgment. Plaintiff 777 Plumbing Inc., cannot recover on any of its claims because its contractor's license was suspended during performance of the work. Business & Professions Code 7125.2 provides that a contractor's license is automatically suspended when a contractor has employees but lacks workers' compensation insurance. The license suspension is effective "[o]n the date that workers' compensation coverage is required to be obtained." (Business & Professions Code 7125.2(a).) When a license is suspended, a contractor lacks the ability to sue for compensation. (Business & Professions Code 7031; Wright v. Isaak (2007) 149 Cal.App.4th 1116.) Defendants satisfied their burden of production showing that Abelardo Sanchez Miranda and Jorge Sanchez were employees of plaintiff's who worked on the Alhambra project and did not have a contractor's license and plaintiff did not have workers' compensation insurance. (UMF 3-6, 8-9, 11-18). Per Labor Code 2750.5 "one performing services requiring a license cannot be an independent contractor unless one holds a valid license. Thus, if one performs work on a construction job for which a license is required, without holding a valid license, one is by definition an employee, not an independent contractor, pursuant to section 2750.5." (Fillmore v. Irvine (1983) 146 Cal.App. 3d 649, 656). Plaintiff fails to present any evidence showing that Abelardo Sanchez Miranda and Jorge Sanchez were not employees per section 2750.5. Nor did plaintiff present any evidence that it substantially complied with the requirement having workers' compensation insurance for its employees on the Alhambra project, even assuming that the doctrine of substantial compliance is applicable in these circumstances. The doctrine of unclean hands is not a bar to the assertion of a section 7031 defense and, even if it was, plaintiff has not presented any evidence that defendants acted with unclean hands with regard to plaintiff's failure to obtain workers' compensation insurance for its workers on the Alhambra project. (Alatriste v. Cesar's Exterior Designs, Inc. (2010) 183 Cal. App. 4th 656, 664-68). Order is signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter; Laura Reding, CSR 711 =(302/hk)

Notice Of Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint And Every Cause Of Action And Allegation Therin Against Defendants John Van Noord, Donna Van Noord And Christiane Gigas
Off Calendar Jun-16-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS IGNACIO CAMARILLO, JOHN VAN NOORD, DONNA VAN NOORD AND CHRISTIANE GIGAS' Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint And Every Cause Of Action And Allegation Therin Against Defendants John Van Noord, Donna Van Noord And Christiane Gigas - Defendants' motion to strike the third cause of action in the complaint is denied. A motion to strike is not the proper procedure to seek adjudication of an entire claim, In all events, the motion to strike is duplicative of the defendants' demurrer. The prevailing party is to prepare a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling and to comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-16-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS IGNACIO CAMARILLO, JOHN VAN NOORD, DONNA VAN NOORD AND CHRISTIANE GIGAS' DEMURRER to the third cause of action is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. If it can do so in good faith, plaintiff 777 Plumbing, Inc. is granted leave to file a new third cause of action labeled "quantum meruit and unjust enrichment" alleging that, with the knowledge of defendants property owners, it provided construction services and furnished construction materials which benefitted the property of the property owners, 777 Plumbing was not paid for some of those services and materials and the property owners did not pay for the services and materials for which 777 Plumbing has not been paid. These allegations sufficiently state a claim and would withstand a demurrer. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
